images.altText: An Emirati patrol boat is near a tanker anchored in the Gulf of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: US denies Iran hit ship in Strait of Hormuz as ceasefire faces major test

children.content: The US military has denied that any of its Navy ships have been hit in the Strait of Hormuz after Iranian state media reported that a US frigate was targeted by two missiles.

image.altText: Two cargo ships can be seen in the distance on blue water, against blue skies. Rocky land is jutting out to sea on the left.

children.content: A large fire has also broken out at the key UAE oil port Fujairah, after it was hit by an Iranian drone attack. Follow live.

items.title: UAE intercepts Iranian missiles for first time since ceasefire

updates.title: Trump issues fresh warning to Iran
Trump claims small boats are all Iran has left
US admiral says six Iranian small boats destroyed

items.title: Holocaust survivor says Jews 'have become targets' in Australia

children.content: The Jewish community's experiences of antisemitism have been put on display on the first day of the royal commission triggered by the Bondi Beach terror attack last year.

image.altText: Peter Halasz wearing glasses and a dark jacket.

items.title: Electric vehicle tax discount to be wound back from next year

children.content: A popular tax discount meaning people leasing electric vehicles did not have to pay Fringe Benefits Tax will be wound back — although in the long term, more EV owners will be able to pay a reduced tax rate.

image.altText: People stand around a car with all its doors open with a sign in beg letters on the wall behind reading BYD.

items.title: The treasurer's reality check on Anthony Albanese's bold childcare plan

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has previously committed to delivering universal child care, but Treasurer Jim Chalmers has highlighted concerns about the cost.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese sits at a table talking with children in a child care class

items.title: Calls for 'compassion' after newborn twin dies at homeless camp

children.content: A women's health advocate says the death of a baby during childbirth at a homeless camp on the Murrumbidgee River is a "terrible day" for the Wagga Wagga community.

image.altText: View of Wagga beach signage from a footbath, looking towards a playground.

items.title: Officers outraged after investigation uncovers taser security flaw

children.content: Police associations and unions demand urgent action over revelations that a security flaw allows officers to be tracked by criminals with a phone or laptop.

articles.title: A hacker built a tool to track police officers. Then he tried to warn them

children.content: A security flaw means the location of devices used by police, potentially including undercover officers and those travelling home, is visible to anyone with a phone or laptop.

image.altText: A man wearing a dark hoodie is silhouetted against the lights of a police station with police cars in front.

gallery.altText: A police officer, wearing a tactical vest with various pieces of equipment including a taser, looks the phone in his hands.

thumbnail.altText: A police officer, wearing a tactical vest with various pieces of equipment including a taser, looks the phone in his hands.

items.title: Two dead as car hits pedestrians in German city of Leipzig

children.content: Authorities say two people are dead after a car slammed into a crowd of people in the eastern German city.

image.altText: Police tape rolled out in front a police van in a large European city square.

items.title: Sydney Lord Mayor Clover Moore cancels 'globalise the intifada' forum

children.content: The event's cancellation is welcomed by groups representing the Jewish community in New South Wales.

image.altText: A woman wearing red sunglasses

items.title: 'He's a big boy': Socceroos boss defends anti-Trump comments from midfielder

children.content: Australia coach Tony Popovic says it would be "wonderful" if Donald Trump was in the stadium in Seattle when the Socceroos played the United States on June 19, saying the US president's presence would motivate his team.

items.title: World order could be 'rebuilt out of Europe', Carney says

children.content: The Canadian prime minister tells European leaders the world does not need to submit to a "more transactional and insular world".

image.altText: A close up of Mark Carney speaking at a press conference.

items.title: Australian businesses falling victim to new type of fraud

children.content: Higher inflation, a war in the Middle East, and a ban on surcharges are crippling small businesses. Now they are falling victim to a modern crime.

children.content: Business journalist Emily Stewart walks you through the elements that will contribute to the RBA's decision to change the cash rate on Tuesday.

children.content: Three people have died and three people are ill after a suspected outbreak of the rodent-borne virus in the Atlantic Ocean.

items.title: Australia and Japan sign agreements on energy, defence and critical minerals

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and his Japanese counterpart Takaichi Sanae agreed to bolster cooperation on energy, defence and critical minerals, while warning against economic coercion.

items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby remembered across NT after 'unthinkable trauma'

children.content: The Northern Territory is continuing to grieve the short life of a five-year-old girl today with crowds in Alice Springs to pay tribute by donning pink, her favourite colour.

items.title: Sports broadcaster Dennis Cometti farewelled at state memorial service

items.title: AFL umpire Nick Foot's betting agency role over after tribunal case

children.content: Betting agency Sportsbet announces it will no longer feature current sports administrators or officials in their programming "following feedback".

image.altText: AFL umpire, Nick Foot, bends down and tenses before throwing the ball in to restart play during a game.

items.title: Russian woman's husband beat her but police were no help, so she wrote to Putin

children.content: Domestic violence and abuse have been described as an "epidemic" in multiple countries, but analysts argue there is something that sets Russia apart: the government is making things worse.

image.altText: Close up of a woman who bears the results of being assaulted, with one eye partly closed.

items.title: Rice is cheap, easy to cook and the main ingredient in these four meals

children.content: Rice doesn't need to play second fiddle. The cheap and simple pantry staple can hold up a whole meal.

children.content: ABC staff have struck an enterprise agreement with the broadcaster, bringing an end to a pay dispute that culminated in a 24-hour strike.

items.title: Netball's 'wishy washy' approach to Pride dulls the message

children.content: For years, the residents of Tasman Drive in Gerringong knew they lived in a stunningly beautiful part of the world, but then social media discovered it too.

items.title: It was a record race time. Moments later spectators were suspicious

children.content: Tasmania's top horse race was run 37 metres shorter than advertised. Officials knew the track distance was likely incorrect but proceeded anyway. This is what happened.

image.altText: Elevated view of horse racing event with crowd looking on.

items.title: Chook left in oven, lost remote among 'absurd' reasons for Triple Zero calls

children.content: Triple Zero call centres are seeing an increase in "absurd" calls, ranging from lost television remote controls to ducks crossing the road.

items.title: Daughter hopes $1m reward will solve mystery of mother's disappearance

children.content: Marion Barter has not been seen since 1997. Her daughter is determined to find out what happened to her mother, who she believes may have found herself "in a situation she did not fully control".

items.title: 'Motivation to swim faster': Four people bitten by crocodile before swimming race

children.content: Four people were bitten by a suspected freshwater crocodile just a day before a sold-out swimming competition was run in the same far-north Western Australia lake.

image.altText: A freshwater crocodile resting next to a lake

items.title: A new poll tells us a lot about One Nation's new-found support

children.content: A new poll asks Australians specifically about One Nation and the results show that rather than being a flash in the pan, the minor party's freight train may be a more lasting phenomenon.

items.title: China accused of targeting Port Pirie and Hobart metal smelters

children.content: South Australia's treasurer and energy minister says China is undermining Australia's smelting industry and that any shutdown would leave national security "very exposed".
